characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The titular protagonist who aspires to become a true hero. The Ace: Once he completes his training under Phil and starts picking up wins over monsters, Hercules quickly develops this status and reputation among the people of Thebes. The "Zero to Hero" musical number is all about his rise to fame and throughout the song, the Muses talk him up as aShowy Invincible Hero, aNice Guy, aChick Magnet, and aHumble Herowith much public love. Adaptational Dumbass: In the myths, Herakles is well educated, a successful military commander by his late teens, an occasional trickster, and a master ofIndy Ploys. The Disney Hercules, while by no means stupid and showing occasional moments of guile, comes off as rather airheaded, naive, klutzy and uncultured in comparison. Adaptational Nice Guy: By modern standards, the Hercules of Greek mythisn't exactly a paragon of heroic virtue. He kills more than one innocent person simply for being too close when his temper gets the better of him (although he's alwaysremorsefulwhen this happens), andhe stages a HUGE war for a mere verbal insult at one point, although he does go to great lengths to help his friends and his deeds do the world a lot of good. This Hercules is a friendly, wide-eyed boy scout who doesn't have many, if any, vices. The worst thing he does is lash out at Phil and slap him for trying to warn him about Meg being in league with Hades, but he immediately comes to regret that. Adaptation Name Change: In the Greek myth, his name is written as "Herakles". "Hercules" is theLatin pronunciation, which is more widely recognizable to modern viewers.

characters.name: Hercules
characters.description: The titular protagonist who aspires to become a true hero. The Ace: Once he completes his training under Phil and starts picking up wins over monsters, Hercules quickly develops this status and reputation among the people of Thebes. The "Zero to Hero" musical number is all about his rise to fame and throughout the song, the Muses talk him up as aShowy Invincible Hero, aNice Guy, aChick Magnet, and aHumble Herowith much public love. Adaptational Dumbass: In the myths, Herakles is well educated, a successful military commander by his late teens, an occasional trickster, and a master ofIndy Ploys. The Disney Hercules, while by no means stupid and showing occasional moments of guile, comes off as rather airheaded, naive, klutzy and uncultured in comparison. Adaptational Nice Guy: By modern standards, the Hercules of Greek mythisn't exactly a paragon of heroic virtue. He kills more than one innocent person simply for being too close when his temper gets the better of him (although he's alwaysremorsefulwhen this happens), andhe stages a HUGE war for a mere verbal insult at one point, although he does go to great lengths to help his friends and his deeds do the world a lot of good. This Hercules is a friendly, wide-eyed boy scout who doesn't have many, if any, vices. The worst thing he does is lash out at Phil and slap him for trying to warn him about Meg being in league with Hades, but he immediately comes to regret that. Adaptation Name Change: In the Greek myth, his name is written as "Herakles". "Hercules" is theLatin pronunciation, which is more widely recognizable to modern viewers.

characters.name: Megara
characters.description: The female lead of the movie. A jaded, sarcastic woman who works under Hades and falls in love with Hercules. Adaptational Backstory Change: In Greek mythology, Megara is the daughter of King Creon of Thebes, making her a princess. There's no mention of Meg being descended from royalty in the film. Adaptational Villainy: Downplayed. The original Megara in Greek myth never worked for Hades, or was associated with any villainous figures in general (though the mythological Hades wasn't evil to start with). Here, she's initially introduced as Hades' disgruntled henchwoman. Age-Gap Romance: Her age isn't specified (unlike Hercules, whose being eighteen is a plot point), but it's implied that she's a fair bit older than him. She refers to him as "junior" when they first meet. The midquel animated series reveals that she's close to his age (she went on a blind date with his classmate, Adonis, and she and Hercules lost their memory of their first meeting when they got accidentally drenched in the Lethe water she enlisted his help in retrieving), but its canonicity is debatable. And in the stage musical, she's already an adult working for Hades when Hercules is just a baby. Annoying Laugh: On seeing Hercules's high school photo, she lets out a cacophonous cackle that Phil calls her out on.

characters.name: Philoctetes
characters.description: A satyr and Hercules' trainer. Adaptation Species Change: He was human in the original myth. Here, he's a satyr. The stage musical reverses this however, and reimagines him as a human gyro seller (or pub employee in later productions). Age Lift: In the original myth, he was a child when Hercules died. Disney's portrayal of him makes him significantly older, enough to have trained many figures of Greek Mythology. Ascended Extra: Philoctetes was barely involved in the original Heracles myth, only playing a part in it after Hercules had died. The Disney movie bumps him up to a major role as Hercules' trainer. Badass Teacher:Downplayed; he teaches Hercules to fight monsters but runs away when monsters actually show up. He's no coward, though — he frequently stays nearby and goads Hercules to keep on fighting like a coach. Phil is smart enough to know that, being the stubby satyr he is, he's completely outmatched by the monsters Herc fights and would be immediately in over his head if he actually tried to fight alongside him. Although even if he can't fight monsters, he's still strong enough to easily beat the crap out ofhumanmen several times his size.

characters.name: Pegasus
characters.description: The flying horse companion of Hercules. Adaptational Backstory Change: The mythological Pegasus was born from the blood of Medusa's decapitated head, and served as the mount for the hero Bellerophon. In this movie, he's created from clouds by Zeus to be a present for the baby Hercules. Animals Lack Attributes: He's meant to be a stallion, as shown by his falling for theHoney Trapmade by Pain and Panic shapeshifting as a mare, but he lacks a sheath or testicles. Also, despite having a prominent butt crack, he lacks an anus, which would have been seen clearly when he was trying to force his butt through Phil's door. Ass Kicks You: He performs mild examples of this a couple of times, which arePlayed for Laughs:After flying Hercules and Meg to the canyon to save the two trapped boys, he hears Meg ask for help getting off lest she “ruin the upholstery” by vomiting, so he bucks his rear end up to force her off into Hercules’ arms.When he and Phil retrieve Hercules from his date with Meg, Hercules is too lovestruck to pay attention and walks backwards while waving bye to her, so Pegasus aims with his hind leg and uses his butt to bounce Hercules onto his back. After flying Hercules and Meg to the canyon to save the two trapped boys, he hears Meg ask for help getting off lest she “ruin the upholstery” by vomiting, so he bucks his rear end up to force her off into Hercules’ arms.

characters.name: Amphitryon and Alcmene
characters.description: Hercules' adoptive parents who raised him after he was taken from his home on Mount Olympus. Adaptational Wimp: The Amphitryon of myth was a famed soldier and a hero in his own right. Here, he's just an elderly farmer, mainly to be an analogue toJonathan Kent. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: At least how Hercules saw them in his teens because they would talk frankly to others of life on the farm instead of tales of adventure or excitement. Cassandra reminds him that every teenager would rather kiss a Gorgon than be seen in public with their parents, but Hercules insists that this is somehow different. Big Ol' Eyebrows: Amphitryon has these and due to age they are also white. Chubby Mama, Skinny Papa: Overweight Alcmene and her skinny husband Amphitryon, respectively.

characters.name: Icarus
characters.description: Hercules' best friend during their time in Prometheus Academy. He also has a romantic obsession with their friend Cassandra. Abhorrent Admirer: To Cassandra. She regards him as a nuisance. Granted, heisbrain damaged, so it's difficult for him to take a hint.Circe took a liking to him, only to lose interest the moment they reached her home. Circe took a liking to him, only to lose interest the moment they reached her home. All Men Are Perverts: His interaction with Cassandra borders on harassment. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: His attention can swap from the topic at hand to the ridiculously mundane at the drop of a hat.

characters.name: Cassandra
characters.description: She is a close friend of Hercules and to a lesser extent Icarus, who is hopelessly in love with her, during their time in Prometheus Academy. Keep in mind,try not to confuse her withtheotherCassandraofanother Disney animated seriesnor thatother onefromanother unrelated fighting game series. Adaptational Nationality: The mythical Cassandra was Trojan (specifically, she was Hector and Paris's sister), not Athenian. Canon Foreigner: Like Icarus up there, she's TV series only. Can't Live with Them, Can't Live Without Them: Icarus may be anAbhorrent Admirer, but Cassandra feels a void when Icarus is gone for alongtime.

characters.name: Adonis
characters.description: A snobbish prince of Thrace and recurring bully for Hercules. Animal Stereotypes: Unsurprisingly, he's turned into a peacock in "Song of Circe". Big Jerk on Campus: A handsome and popularRoyal Bratwho enjoys making fun of Hercules and anyone else he deems below him.Even Herc has a hard time standing him. The Bully: He enjoyed humiliating Hercules and his friends during their time at Prometheus Academy. Bullying a Dragon: In the series, it's common knowledge that Herc's the son of Zeus, and that he has super strength. Why he still bullies Herc is anyone's guess.

characters.name: Helen of Troy
characters.description: The most popular girl at Prometheus Academy. All Girls Want Bad Boys: She has a notable attraction to powerful or unpleasant men, as seen with her constant relationship with Adonis despite being a jerkass snob and a later stated attraction to Orion the Hunter for his macho and hairy persona. She does provide affection to Hercules though not enough to formally be with him in a relationship. Brainless Beauty: Helen of Troy. Her face launched a thousand ships, not her head. Class Princess: A nice and friendly cheerleader. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Helen has blonde hair and is an absolute sweetheart.

characters.name: Daedalus
characters.description: The shop class teacher at Prometheus Academy, famed inventor, and Icarus' father. He is far wiser than his son, though about as eccentric. Absent-Minded Professor: Daedalus is incredibly intelligent and sharp, but when he gets to inventing everything else around him tends to vanish. This allows villains like Minos to get him to make them something dangerous without Daedalus questioning them until its too late. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Aloof and often impatient, he still wants his students to succeed. In particular he will get fed up and impatient with Hercules, but refuses to give up on him and wants to help him learn. Papa Wolf: He is incredibly protective of Icarus, as well as Hercules. Minos finds this out the hard way.

characters.name: Orpheus
characters.description: The hottest singer in Greece, Orpheus is aTeen Idolof considerable renown who frequently plays sold-out concerts. He was mentioned offhand in the movie before appearing in person in the animated series. Adapted Out: There's no mention of the other famous half of his myth: his lover Eurydice. British Rockstar: Orpheus speaks with an English accent and is the most popular singer in Greece. Hidden Depths: According to Hermes in the movie, he's also good at flower arrangements. Rescued from the Underworld: In a twist on the trope maker, Orpheus himself has to be rescued when Hades kidnaps him for a concert.

characters.name: Tempest
characters.description: A warrior and princess of the Amazons. The Ace: A very skilled Amazonian warrior, who is able to throw a spear. Action Girl: It's not uncommon to see her accompanying Hercules (or Hercules being in her way) and doing a better job in tasks. All Amazons Want Hercules: Averted, as she's never shown romantic feelings towards Hercules. In fact, when she first meets him in "Hercules and the Assassin", she's unimpressed and doesn't open up to him (though this is mostly due to her strict upbringing). By the end of the episode, she shows respects towards him. Anime Hair: Her ponytail is shaped like the horsehair crest on top of a helmet, which showcases her war-like personality.

characters.name: Chipacles
characters.description: Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He is a blunt and stern cop who is still a sincerely good guy. He is just as likely to help Herc as get in his way. Pet the Dog: Chipalces may get on Herc's case a lot, but he shows frequently he can lighten up and be aReasonable Authority Figure.InHercules and the Driving Test, Hercules basically stops the sun to give himself more time to complete his driving test. Chipacles is perfectly okay with this unusual loophole and is willing to let Hercules finish the test.Chiplaces is ready to punish Hercules for sneaking into a tavern despite being underaged for most ofHercules and the Epic Adventure, but after Hercules saves the city from Orthos he is willing to look the other way.He is quick to team up with both Herc and Phil inHercules and Disappearing Heroes, even letting Hercules take the lead when he gets a good plan. At the end of the episode he is seen bowling with them now, admitting he thinks they are okay in his book now. InHercules and the Driving Test, Hercules basically stops the sun to give himself more time to complete his driving test. Chipacles is perfectly okay with this unusual loophole and is willing to let Hercules finish the test. Chiplaces is ready to punish Hercules for sneaking into a tavern despite being underaged for most ofHercules and the Epic Adventure, but after Hercules saves the city from Orthos he is willing to look the other way. He is quick to team up with both Herc and Phil inHercules and Disappearing Heroes, even letting Hercules take the lead when he gets a good plan. At the end of the episode he is seen bowling with them now, admitting he thinks they are okay in his book now.

characters.name: Chiron
characters.description: Adaptational Jerkass: Chiron in the original Greek myth was famous for being the only gentle centaur. Here, Chiron acts just as aggressively and arrogantly as all the other centaurs we see. Always Someone Better: It is initially believed he is the better hero trainer than Phil is. This is ultimately subverted though; Phil's star student ultimately proves he is far better than evenChiron himself.

characters.name: Bob the Narrator
characters.description: A mysterious, presumably mortal voice that narrates the adventures of Hercules and friends. Bob is on good terms with the Muses despite their frequent narrative takeovers, and has a wife named Mrs. Bob, a daughter named Tiffany and a son named Chad. Adam Westing: His name Bob is a shortened nickname for Robert, implying that the show's narrator really is Robert Stack interacting with the Greek world. How this connects to the Charlton Heston narrator from the movie, if it does at all, is unknown. Ascended Extra: The Narrator in the movie serves as more of a gag to introduce the muses. He only has a few lines before they take over and isn't heard from after he hands the story off to them. In the series, he has much more extended interactions with the muses and frequently covers smaller bits of exposition. Bob usually has more lines in an episode than he did in the entire movie. The Fourth Wall Will Not Protect You:Played for Laughswhen Circe hypnotizes Bob to stop him from pointing out how evil and manipulative she is and switch it so that he's complimenting her. Lemony Narrator: Bob often interacts with the Muses in the episode openings.

characters.name: Odysseus
characters.description: King of Ithaca and Hercules’ most favorite hero that he looks up to and aspires to be. He was a former student of Phil. Adaptational Angst Downgrade: Telemachus isn’t mentioned in the opening narration and Odysseus himself admits to Hercules that his son is around the latter’s age, i.e late teens. This means he became a parentafterreturning home and got to be a part of his son’s life from the get-go. There’s also no mention of him ever gettingimprisoned by Circe and/or Calypso. Babies Ever After: After reuniting with Penelope, they went on to have Telemachus. Badass in Distress: Hercules accidentally trapping him into Hecate’s clutches leads to him getting imprisoned and his cunning stolen away. Crazy Jealous Guy: Odysseus isnotpleased when Penelope admits that she had a lot of suitors pursuing her while he was away from home. When he angrily questions why he’s only finding out about it now, Penelope reveals that he already killed them, to which Odysseus admits he thought they were salesmen.

characters.name: Cerberus
characters.description: Hades' giant three-headed dog, who guards the entrance to the Underworld. Adaptation Expansion: Not an enormous one, but he gets more screentime in the animated series as a puppy. He also appears as a reoccuring boss in theKingdom Heartsseries. Angry Guard Dog: He's anenormous dogwho guards the entrance to the Underworld. Beware of Vicious Dog: He actually snarled at Hades as he entered, though he is later pacified when being given a large piece of steak (that’s tiny from their perspective) by Hades. Canis Major:Oh boy is he!He's even larger thanClifford!

characters.name: Nessus
characters.description: A centaur whom Hades sent Megara to recruit. Abhorrent Admirer: He has hots for Megara, who obviously doesn't reciprocate. Adaptational Wimp: Downplayed. The mythological Nessus was the one responsible for Heracles' death thanks to the centaur's poisonous blood, but it was aMutual Kill, and Heracles only died shortly after killing Nessus. In the film proper, he gets only one good hit in before Herculescurb-stomps him. Amazing Technicolor Population: Nessus has blue skin a few shades lighter than the fur covering his lower body. Cranial Eruption: He's left with a massive welt on his head, which his horseshoes land on.

characters.name: Hydra
characters.description: A two-legged serpentine dragon with the ability to grow back multiple heads for every one that's cut off. 2D Visuals, 3D Effects: It's animated with CG instead of traditional 2D animation. Alien Blood: Hercules decapitates the Hydra dozens of times during their battle, which is shown in full, unusually gory detail for a Disney movie. But since the Hydra happens to have green blood, the scene is still family-friendly despite the violence depicted. Ambiguous Gender: Most sources refer to the Hydra as "it". Phil's dialogue in the film and read-along storybook refers to the Hydra with masculine pronouns ("Dance around him!"), and he suggests calling it "sir" inDisney's Animated Storybook. However, there is a single instance in theClassic Storybookjunior novelization (which otherwise uses "it") where the Hydra is referred to as "her", suggesting that it is actuallyfemale; this is not the case in theLadybirdnovelization, despite the two books sharing much of the same text. Animal Assassin: Giant man-eating mythological monster version. Unlike his other monstrous minions, Hades doesn't apparently control the Hydra but merely creates circumstances where Hercules is bound to release and encounter the beast, with its hunger taking care of the rest. The effect is much the same though: if Hercules hadn't slain the Hydra first, his death wouldn't have looked like a murder to the other gods.

characters.name: Echidna
characters.description: A draconic Titan who is the wife of Typhon and mother of several powerful monsters. Adaptational Ugliness: Descriptions of Echidna in mythology vary, but she's often said to look like a beautiful nymph with the body of a serpent or dragon. Here, she's a monstrously hideous kaiju like her husband, though shedoeskeep the serpentine dragon motif. Beware My Stinger Tail: Her tail is tipped by a scorpion-like stinger, which Hercules uses to his advantage by forcing her to sting herself.Echidna:[snarls in pain]Watch where you point that thing! Card-Carrying Villain: Echidna is a proud monster and delights in causing death, destruction, and chaos; wanting her children to follow in her footsteps. Draconic Abomination: Echidna is an ancient draconic goddess with four legs, two arms, a mane of fur covering her torso, horns, a pair of small wings, and a humanoid face.

characters.name: Typhon
characters.description: A draconic Titan who once fought Zeus for control of the cosmos and was trapped beneath a mountain. Arch-Enemy: Typhon was this to Zeus long before Hades became consumed by envy. Draconic Abomination: Typhon is a five-headed draconic monster — with four heads serving as arms — with tentacles for legs, and is an ancient and powerful monster who once battled Zeus for control of the cosmos. The Dreaded: True to the mythology, Typhon is feared by mortals and gods alike. When he’s freed, the reaction from the Athenians is aMass "Oh, Crap!". Happily Married: Typhon is married to fellow draconic Titan Echidna, with whom he had several monstrous offspring.

characters.name: Medusa
characters.description: A Gorgon who lives in Gorgon Land. Despite her monstrous appearance, Medusa is a nice, insecure girl who just wants to have friends. Adaptational Nice Girl: Depending on which myth you believe, Medusa was born a Gorgon and she enjoyed petrifying people. Here, she feels lonely and just wants real friends. Become a Real Boy: Between Hades' offer to turn into a human at daylight and Aphrodite's offer to wear a pair of magical glasses which won't turn people into stone, Medusa chooses the former. Cute Monster Girl: Even though she believes she's hideous, her human-like characteristics and her desire to have friends makes her seem cute. Deadly Gaze: Her gaze turns everyone into stone.

characters.name: Orthos
characters.description: A two-headed cyclops who became Hercules' first enemy. Adaptation Species Change: In the original Greek myth, Orthos was a two-headed dog who guarded Geryon's cattle and the brother of his three-headed counterpart Cerberus. Here, he's a two-headed cyclops. Blue Means Cold: His skin color is blue. Cyclops: A cyclops with two heads. Diet Episode: In their debute episode, Echidna is tired of their slovenly lifestyle and puts them on a strict diet of only eating royalty or heroes to get them out of their lair and get in shape.

characters.name: The Sphinx
characters.description: Exclusive to theHercules Dynamitecomics, the Sphinx is a terrifying, huge winged cat monster more dangerous than the Titans. It took every god, including Hades, to exile her to Egypt, but she learned ancient arts there and returned to kidnap the pantheon one by one, draining their powers and establishing her own rule. All Your Powers Combined: After draining the gods' power she can make use of all of them, and does so. Came Back Strong: She learned ancient magic during her exile to Egypt, which made her stronger than ever and a completely unknown force to the Greek Gods. The Dreaded: So evil and powerful that all the gods fear her, and it took all of them to defeat her the last time. Eviler than Thou: Stronger than the Titans and evil enough for Hades to team up with the pantheon to defeat her.
